title: santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. valida
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. valida is a subvariety of the gray santolina, a shrubby perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It is also known as lavender cotton or cotton lavender, due to its small, cotton-like flower heads that resemble lavender blooms. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. valida include relieving digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting respiratory health, and it can be prepared into teas, infusions, and salves for topical use.
